channel.title: NYT > World > Middle East
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Israel Says Netanyahu Made a Secret Trip to U.A.E., Which Says He Didn’t
item.description: The claim and the denial signaled both how close Israel and the United Arab Emirates have become, and how delicate that relationship remains.
item.dc:creator: Pranav Baskar and Johnatan Reiss
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu of Israel in Jerusalem in April.

item.title: Gen. Caine’s Silence on Iran War Leaves Questions About Military Strategy
item.description: The president’s top military adviser is walking a tightrope as he leads the military through a divisive and unpopular war.
item.dc:creator: Greg Jaffe
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: In public, Gen. Dan Caine has avoided discussing with lawmakers the broader U.S. military strategy.

item.title: Top Gaza Negotiator Urges Hamas to Embrace Rebuilding Plan
item.description: Nickolay Mladenov, who is overseeing the U.S.-led truce in Gaza, has tried to convince the militant group to give up its arms, but it has so far refused amid Israeli cease-fire violations.
item.dc:creator: David M. Halbfinger, Adam Rasgon and Aaron Boxerman
item.media:credit: Saher Alghorra for The New York Times
item.media:description: Members of Hamas in Gaza City in November.

item.title: Chinese Firms Plot Secret Arms Sales to Iran, U.S. Officials Say
item.description: The effort involves plans to send weapons through other countries in an effort to hide the origins of the shipments.
item.dc:creator: Julian E. Barnes, Mark Mazzetti and Dustin Volz
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: President Trump arrived in Beijing on Wednesday for a high-stakes diplomatic visit with President Xi Jinping of China.

item.title: For Trump, the Iran War Looms Over China Summit
item.description: The balance of power between the United States and China had shifted in Beijing’s favor even before the war in Iran began in February.
item.dc:creator: Anton Troianovski
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: President Donald J. Trump leaving the White House on Tuesday. He arrived in Beijing today.

item.title: Why the Bombing of Iran Tied the U.S. More Closely to China
item.description: As the U.S. tries to rebuild its weapons stockpiles drained in the Iran war, it will need access to rare-earth minerals, an industry China dominates.
item.dc:creator: Ana Swanson and John Ismay
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: A destroyed building in Tehran last month. The war in Iran is expected to figure into talks between the United States and China in Beijing this week.

item.title: At Cannes 2026, Gaza Becomes a Political Flashpoint
item.description: The event’s leaders immediately jumped into the debate over whether festival participants should speak out on issues like Israel and Gaza.
item.dc:creator: Kyle Buchanan
item.media:description: From left, the Cannes Film Festival president, Iris Knobloch, the jury president, Peter Jackson, and the festival director, Thierry Frémaux.

item.title: Trump Is in China as Iran War Continues With No End in Sight
item.description: Before leaving Washington on Tuesday, the president reiterated threats to decimate Iran if it doesn’t agree to a deal to resolve the conflict.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Boxerman
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: President Trump departing Joint Base Andrews in Maryland for China on Tuesday.

item.title: Gulf Countries Arrest Shiite ‘Traitors’ Amid War With Iran
item.description: Dozens of Gulf citizens have been accused of belonging to Iran-linked terrorism cells as the war accelerates a shift toward deeper authoritarianism in the region.
item.dc:creator: Vivian Nereim
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Smoke billowing after a drone strike in Kuwait City in March.

item.title: Battle Over the Strait of Hormuz Leaves Safe Passage a Gamble
item.description: Vessels are traveling without transmitting critical information meant to keep the waters safe, maritime intelligence experts say.
item.dc:creator: Ephrat Livni and Euan Ward
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: Ships in the Strait of Hormuz near Oman on Friday.

item.title: Israeli Report Finds That Sexual Violence by Hamas Was Widespread
item.description: A two-year investigation by a team of researchers in Israel concluded that sexual violence by Hamas and its allies was widespread during and after the Oct. 7, 2023, attack.
item.dc:creator: Isabel Kershner
item.category: War Crimes, Genocide and Crimes Against Humanity
item.media:credit: Avishag Shaar-Yashuv for The New York Times
item.media:description: A house in kibbutz Kfar Aza, Israel, that was among those attacked on Oct. 7, 2023.

item.title: Earthquake Shakes Tehran, Where Nerves Are Already Strained by Iran War
item.description: Iranian media said a moderate quake had shaken the capital around midnight local time.
item.dc:creator: Farnaz Fassihi
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: U.S. Intelligence Shows Iran Retains Substantial Missile Capabilities
item.description: Secret new assessments say Iran has operational access to 30 of its 33 missile sites along the Strait of Hormuz, suggesting that its military remains far stronger than President Trump has asserted.
item.dc:creator: Adam Entous, Maggie Haberman and Jonathan Swan
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: Missile displays in Tehran in 2024. According to classified U.S. intelligence assessments, Iran retains roughly 70 percent of its prewar missile stockpile.

item.title: U.S. Might Restart Strikes on Iran, Trump and Hegseth Warn
item.description: With talks at an impasse, both sides are holding to positions their opponents call unacceptable, while the economic pain caused by the Strait of Hormuz blockade continues to grow.
item.dc:creator: Karoun Demirjian, Megan Mineiro, Euan Ward and Michael Levenson
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: “We’re either going to make a deal or they’re going to be decimated,” President Trump told reporters at the White House before departing for China on Tuesday.

item.title: Kuwait Accuses Iran of Trying to Infiltrate Its Territory
item.description: Kuwait’s interior ministry said a group of armed Iranians arrived at Bubiyan Island in the Persian Gulf on May 1 aboard a rented fishing boat and exchanged fire with Kuwaiti soldiers.
item.dc:creator: Ismaeel Naar
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Kuwait says it arrested four members of the Islamic Revolutionary Guards Corps who arrived on a boat.

item.title: Pentagon Puts Iran War Cost at $29 Billion as Hegseth Deflects on Funding
item.description: The estimate from the Defense Department’s top budget official was $4 billion higher than two weeks ago. Secretary Pete Hegseth declined to say when the administration would request extra money for the Iran conflict.
item.dc:creator: Megan Mineiro
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: From left, Jay Hurst, the Pentagon comptroller; Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth; and Gen. Dan Caine testified about the Defense Department’s budget request on Tuesday.

item.title: Here’s the latest.
item.dc:creator: Max Bearak, Karoun Demirjian and Megan Mineiro

item.title: Inflation Jumps as War-Driven Energy Costs Drive Up Prices
item.description: President Trump has declared the cease-fire on “life support,” and investors are taking a cautious approach as the economic effects of the war become more apparent.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Vessels in the Strait of Hormuz on Friday. Iran has effectively shut down the narrow waterway.

item.title: Former Hostage Calls on All of Israel’s Government to Resign
item.description: Rom Braslavski, who said he had endured starvation, torture and sexual abuse during two-year captivity, demanded that members “take responsibility and get out of our lives.”
item.dc:creator: Ephrat Livni
item.media:description: Rom Braslavski, in glasses, with three other former hostages during a ceremony in Tel Aviv in January marking the return of Israel’s last remaining hostage.

item.title: As Trump Dismisses Iran Truce Plan, World Braces for Lasting Economic Pain
item.description: President Trump said the fragile cease-fire was on “life support,” and India’s leader urged residents to cut back on purchases and travel.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Boxerman, Sanam Mahoozi, Michael Levenson and Max Bearak
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: Tehran last week. Iranian officials are facing domestic pressure to reach a deal as the country’s economic problems have grown only more dire since the war.

item.title: Israel Prepares to Prosecute First Suspects in Oct. 7 Attack Led by Hamas
item.description: Parliament passed a new law that paves the way for military trials for hundreds of Palestinians suspected of taking part in the 2023 attack that ignited the two-year war in Gaza.
item.dc:creator: Johnatan Reiss
item.category: War Crimes, Genocide and Crimes Against Humanity
item.media:description: Palestinians wave their national flag and celebrate by a destroyed Israeli tank in southern Gaza on Oct. 7, 2023.

item.title: Trump Proposes Suspending Federal Gas Tax Until Prices Fall
item.description: Congress would have to agree to any suspension, and dropping federal taxes would not make up for the steep increase in prices since the war began.
item.dc:creator: Shawn McCreesh
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Gas prices have risen about 50 percent since the United States and Israel began the war with Iran on Feb. 28.

item.title: Narges Mohammadi, Jailed Iranian Nobel Prize Winner Is Transferred to Tehran Hospital
item.description: Narges Mohammadi, a prominent human rights activist who has spent much of her adult life in and out of prison, will undergo treatment in Tehran after collapsing earlier this month, a foundation run by her family said.
item.dc:creator: Amelia Nierenberg
item.category: Human Rights and Human Rights Violations
item.media:credit: The Yomiuri Shimbun, via Associated Press
item.media:description: Narges Mohammadi, an Iranian human rights activist, last year. She received the Nobel Peace Prize in 2023 for her advocacy of women’s rights in her country.

item.title: Iran defends its demands after Trump’s rejection.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Boxerman and Sanam Mahoozi
item.media:credit: Foad Ashtari/SOPA Images, via Getty Images
item.media:description: Esmail Baghaei, the spokesman for Iran’s Ministry of Foreign Affairs, in Tehran last month.

item.title: Here’s the latest.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Boxerman and Max Bearak

item.title: Trump Says Iran’s Response to Latest U.S. Proposal ‘Totally Unacceptable’
item.description: The United States and Iran have been discussing a 30-day extension to their cease-fire and a reopening of the Strait of Hormuz.
item.dc:creator: Erica L. Green, Aaron Boxerman and Adam Sella
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: Tehran on Saturday. Iran gave its response through Pakistan to an American proposal to end the conflict, Iranian state media reported.

item.title: Lebanese Mourn Eight Members of One Family Killed in Israeli Strike
item.description: Israel has escalated attacks on Lebanon since Thursday, further unraveling a truce in its war with Hezbollah. Among those killed on Saturday was a 6-month-old infant, relatives said.
item.dc:creator: Abdi Latif Dahir
item.media:description: Mourners paying their respects to the eight family members who were killed in an Israeli strike on southern Lebanon.

item.title: Mass Layoffs in Iran as Businesses Buckle Under Wartime Pressures
item.description: Iran was already struggling economically before 2026 brought widespread instability. A government-imposed internet shutdown has crippled an entire sector.
item.dc:creator: Leily Nikounazar and Arash Khamooshi
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Crowds inside the sprawling Grand Bazaar in Tehran on Saturday. Imports of goods have been affected by the war.

item.title: Strait of Hormuz Remains Effectively Blocked After Naval Skirmishes
item.description: The U.S. Navy’s blockade continued to intercept ships trying to leave or enter Iranian ports, while fear of Iranian attacks kept other ships from attempting passage through the narrows.
item.dc:creator: Pranav Baskar and Devon Lum
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: Tankers anchored in the Strait of Hormuz off the coast of Qeshm Island, Iran, last month.

item.title: Deadly Israeli Strikes Erode Cease-Fire in Lebanon
item.description: Fighting between Israel and Hezbollah has shown little sign of abating, with Hezbollah also continuing to attack Israeli troops, and both sides accusing the other of violating a U.S.-brokered truce.
item.dc:creator: Euan Ward and Abdi Latif Dahir
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Rescue workers searching for survivors under the rubble of houses damaged by an Israeli airstrike Saturday, in the village of Saksakiyeh in southern Lebanon.

item.title: Here’s the latest.
Long Overlooked, Caspian Sea Provides Strategic Trade Route for Iran
item.description: The landlocked body of water has taken on new significance, with Russia shipping military and commercial goods to bolster Tehran’s ability to withstand the U.S. assault.
item.dc:creator: Nicholas Kulish, Neil MacFarquhar and Julian E. Barnes
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: A woman walks along the southern coast of the Caspian Sea in the port city of Bandar Anzali, Iran. The Caspian has emerged as a key trade route between Russia and Iran.

item.title: Iran Says It Seized Oil Tanker Listed by U.S. as Sanctions Violator
item.description: A maritime tracking company said the move was likely performative, given the Chinese-owned ship has an “established history within the Iranian trade ecosystem.”
item.dc:creator: Jenny Gross
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Ships in the Strait of Hormuz near Oman on Friday.

item.title: Emirates Doubles Down on U.S. and Israeli Ties Amid Iran War
item.description: The United Arab Emirates has borne the brunt of Iranian attacks throughout the war, hardening the government’s resolve to reassess its list of friends and foes.
item.dc:creator: Vivian Nereim
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: The Emirates leader, Sheikh Mohammed bin Zayed, and President Trump in Abu Dhabi, last year.

item.title: Artists’ Strike Closes Pavilions at Venice Biennale, Adding to Upheaval
item.description: The hottest exhibitions at the world’s major art exhibition were shuttered on Friday as part of a pro-Palestinian demonstration.
item.dc:creator: Alex Marshall
item.media:credit: Casey Kelbaugh for The New York Times
item.media:description: The Netherlands’ shuttered Venice Biennale pavilion displayed a flag and fliers in support of Palestinians on Friday.

item.title: Here’s the latest.
item.dc:creator: Farnaz Fassihi, Erica L. Green, Motoko Rich and Euan Ward

item.title: Trump Says Cease-Fire Is Intact After Trade of Attacks in Strait of Hormuz
item.description: The president dismissed Iran’s firing on U.S. vessels in the strait as a mere “trifle.”
item.dc:creator: Erica L. Green
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: President Trump at the Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool on the National Mall in Washington, D.C., on Thursday.

item.title: U.S. and Iran Trade Strikes as Tensions in Strait of Hormuz Rise
item.description: The escalating attacks raise the threat that the cease-fire could break down.
item.dc:creator: Helene Cooper
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: This photo released by U.S. Central Command Public Affairs shows the aircraft carrier U.S.S. George H.W. Bush on Sunday.

item.title: As Stockpiles Fall, U.S. Sells More Missiles Worth $17 Billion to Gulf Nations
item.description: The United States and its partners have burned through an enormous amount of air defense missiles in the Iran war, and production is slow.
item.dc:creator: Edward Wong
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: A Patriot missile launcher in Kuwait City in 2019. The United States is selling Kuwait another $9.3 billion worth of interceptor missiles.

item.title: Iran and U.S. Consider One-Page Plan to End Hostilities, Iranian Officials Say
item.description: The plan would reopen the Strait of Hormuz and give the two countries 30 days to hammer out a comprehensive deal, according to three Iranian officials.
item.dc:creator: Farnaz Fassihi
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: A vessel in the Strait of Hormuz on Wednesday near Fujairah, United Arab Emirates.

item.title: Israel Says It Killed a Hezbollah Chief Near Beirut, Testing the Truce
item.description: The strike was the first near the Lebanese capital since a cease-fire that has curbed fighting but not halted it. Washington is pushing for a lasting peace, hoping it will ease diplomacy with Iran.
item.dc:creator: Euan Ward
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Rescue workers on Thursday at a building in Beirut’s southern suburbs that was destroyed a day earlier by an Israeli strike.

item.title: House Republican Proposes Bill to Wind Down the Iran War
item.description: Representative Tom Barrett, a Michigan Republican facing a tough re-election race, introduced a bill to impose limits on the use of military force in Iran and end the fighting this summer.
item.dc:creator: Robert Jimison
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Alfield Reeves for The New York Times
item.media:description: Representative Tom Barrett at a rally in 2024.

item.title: Shell Reports Nearly $7 Billion Profit After Oil Prices Surged Amid U.S.-Iran War
item.description: The oil giant’s earnings in the first three months of the year were more than double the previous quarter’s and follow similarly strong results of European rivals.
item.dc:creator: Gregory Schmidt and Rebecca F. Elliott
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Shell reported huge profits for the oil company in the first three months of the year.

item.title: U.S. Awaits Iran’s Response to Latest Peace Proposal to End War
item.dc:creator: Farnaz Fassihi, Pranav Baskar, Helene Cooper and Erica L. Green
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
